other_observers: None; I found and identified the bird. Brent Thordarson and Kevin Keirn saw the bird on the same afternoon but later in the day.
specific_location: N. Poudre #3 on Larimer County Rd. 11 between Rd. 66 & 68
description: White face and forehead. The mid-crown back to the nape was black. Black mask extended from eye across mid-crown to nape. Some black streaking on central crown. Small black bill. While sitting the tail appeared shorter than the wingtips and the upperparts appeared an even pale gray. Very short black legs, which were difficult to see when the bird was standing. Rounded belly almost touching the ground. In flight the underparts were lighter than the mantle and wings, at times appearing almost white. Tail was white, forked and short. Secondaries were white and appeared translucent in direct sunlight. The primaries were translucent except for the very tips, which were black. A faint carpal bar was present. There was no black trailing edge to the wing.Behaviors: The tern was actively flying, hovering and diving for several minutes at a time. It would often go and stand on a sand spit for short periods and then resume its feeding and hunting behavior.Call: Not heardPlumage: 2nd cycle
